title: Uragoga guineensis

intro: Uragoga guineensis, also known as African bitter leaf or Onugbu, is a plant species native to tropical Africa, particularly in the regions of Guinea, Nigeria, and Cameroon. The health benefits of Uragoga guineensis include its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, diarrhea, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional African medicine, Uragoga guineensis is often prepared as a tea, decoction, or infusion, and is commonly used to treat various ailments, including fever, digestive issues, and menstrual disorders.
